title: Zillow Posts Profit in 2025 as Mortgage and Rentals Drive Growth
text: Zillow Rental Business and Multifamily Revenue Growth. Recent Performance (Q1 2026): Rentals revenue increased 42% year over year to $183 million in Q1 2026, primarily driven by multifamily revenue growing 57% year over year. 2025 Full Year and Recent Quarters: Rentals revenue rose 45% annually in Q4 2025 to $168 million, and 39% for the entire year, clocking in at $630 million. More specifically, rentals revenue increased 45% year over year to $168 million in Q4, primarily driven by multifamily revenue growing 63% year over year. In Q3 2025, rentals revenue of $174 million represented 41% year-over-year growth, with multifamily revenue rising 62% year over year. Multifamily Expansion: Multifamily properties grew 44% in 2025 to 72,000, against an addressable market of 140,000 buildings, with 2026 guidance of approximately 30% Rentals revenue growth. Strategic Significance: Rentals remained the fastest-growing segment of revenue for the company, jumping 45 percent from a year earlier, to $168 million in the quarter. The growth in multifamily rental revenue has been a major driver of Zillow's overall business acceleration, with the company achieving full-year GAAP profitability.
